core_mechanism: a two-species reaction-diffusion (Gray-Scott activator-substrate) system whose diffusion-driven (Turing-type) instability produces a multistable landscape of many coexisting stable spatial-pattern steady states rather than a single fixed point
brief_reason: The abstract adapts the Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion model of two interacting diffusing species and reports numerical discovery of numerous distinct stable steady-state spatial patterns, i.e. multistable diffusion-driven pattern formation.
